description: Passphrase-KDF registry: the key-derivation functions selectable on the wire via an encryption envelope's `passphrase.alg` field, used to stretch a human passphrase into a content-encryption key for the passphrase key-path (the alternative to recipient KEM slots). The pinned `params` carry the closed parameter set and security floors the structural validator enforces; values below the floor are ENC_PASSPHRASE_ARGON2_PARAMS_TOO_LOW, an out-of-range salt is ENC_PASSPHRASE_SALT_TOO_SHORT or ENC_PASSPHRASE_SALT_TOO_LONG, an unknown params field is SCHEMA_UNKNOWN_FIELD, and an unregistered identifier is ENC_PASSPHRASE_ALG_UNSUPPORTED. Note: HKDF-SHA-256 and HMAC-SHA-256 are FIXED constructions used internally (slot key derivation, MACs) and carry no wire identifier — they are not selectable and are not entries here. PBKDF2-SHA-256 is intentionally excluded: it belongs to the out-of-scope identity key-envelope, not to this wire standard. Additions are additive-only and must ship with conformance vectors.

entries.description: Argon2id memory-hard password-hashing function. The sole wire-selectable passphrase KDF for the passphrase key-path. The validator enforces a closed parameter set {m, t, p} with minimum floors and a bounded salt length.
entries.reference: RFC 9106 (Argon2 Memory-Hard Function for Password Hashing and Proof-of-Work)
